puremoe 1.0.3
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started
    • MeSH-Guided Search
  • Changelog

MeSH-Guided Search

Source: vignettes/mesh-search.Rmd
mesh-search.Rmd

PubMed indexes articles with Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) – a controlled vocabulary maintained by NLM. A [MeSH Terms] query retrieves articles explicitly tagged with a concept regardless of how the authors phrased it, and triggers explosion: all narrower terms in the hierarchy are included automatically. puremoe ships two MeSH reference datasets, the thesaurus and the tree, that make it straightforward to identify the right headings before running a search.

Load packages.

library(puremoe)
library(data.table)
library(DT)

MeSH thesaurus

data_mesh_thesaurus() downloads and combines the MeSH Descriptor Thesaurus and Supplementary Concept Records (SCR). It returns one row per term, including all synonyms and entry terms for each descriptor.

thesaurus <- puremoe::data_mesh_thesaurus()
dim(thesaurus)
#> [1] 987271      9
names(thesaurus)
#> [1] "DescriptorUI"           "DescriptorName"         "ConceptUI"             
#> [4] "TermUI"                 "TermName"               "ConceptPreferredTermYN"
#> [7] "IsPermutedTermYN"       "LexicalTag"             "RecordPreferredTermYN"
head(thesaurus, 6) |> DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Finding terms related to a topic

Search the thesaurus for headings related to political ideology.

hits <- thesaurus[grepl("politi", TermName, ignore.case = TRUE)]
hits[, .(DescriptorName, TermName)] |>
  unique() |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Pull out the canonical descriptor names – one row per MeSH heading.

descriptors <- hits[, .(DescriptorName)] |> unique()
descriptors
#>                    DescriptorName
#>                            <char>
#> 1:             Political Activism
#> 2:              Political Systems
#> 3:                       Politics
#> 4:                       Refugees
#> 5:           Dissent and Disputes
#> 6:       Rhodococcus kronopolitis
#> 7:      Streptomyces kronopolitis
#> 8: Streptosporangium kronopolitis

MeSH trees

data_mesh_trees() provides the hierarchical classification structure. Each descriptor can appear in multiple branches; tree_location encodes the full path (e.g., I01.880.604 = Social Sciences > Political Science > Political Systems).

trees <- puremoe::data_mesh_trees()
dim(trees)
#> [1] 64342     9
names(trees)
#> [1] "DescriptorUI"   "DescriptorName" "tree_location"  "code"          
#> [5] "cats"           "mesh1"          "mesh2"          "tree1"         
#> [9] "tree2"
head(trees, 6) |> DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Locating descriptors in the hierarchy

Find where our candidate descriptors sit.

political_trees <- trees[DescriptorName %in% descriptors$DescriptorName]
political_trees[, .(DescriptorName, tree_location, cats)] |>
  unique() |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Exploring a branch

Retrieve everything in the I01.880 subtree (Political Science and related concepts).

branch <- trees[grepl("^I01\\.880", tree_location)]
branch[, .(DescriptorName, tree_location)][order(tree_location)] |>
  unique() |>
  head(20) |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Siblings and parent context

Look at all direct children of I01 (Social Sciences) – one dot of depth below the parent.

siblings <- trees[grepl("^I01\\.[^.]+$", tree_location)]
siblings[, .(DescriptorName, tree_location)][order(tree_location)] |>
  unique() |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Building a MeSH query

Construct a controlled-vocabulary PubMed query from the descriptors identified above. [MeSH Terms] triggers explosion; use [MeSH:noexp] to suppress it.

mesh_terms <- c("Politics", "Political Systems")
mesh_query <- paste(sprintf('"%s"[MeSH Terms]', mesh_terms), collapse = " OR ")
cat(mesh_query)
#> "Politics"[MeSH Terms] OR "Political Systems"[MeSH Terms]

Combine with a free-text component to capture articles not yet indexed or tagged under a different heading.

combined_query <- paste0(
  '(', mesh_query, ')',
  ' OR ',
  '("political ideology"[TiAb])'
)
cat(combined_query)
#> ("Politics"[MeSH Terms] OR "Political Systems"[MeSH Terms]) OR ("political ideology"[TiAb])

Comparing queries

Run both queries and compare recall.

pmids_mesh     <- puremoe::search_pubmed(mesh_query)
pmids_combined <- puremoe::search_pubmed(combined_query)

data.table(
  query   = c("MeSH only", "Combined (MeSH + free-text)"),
  n_pmids = c(length(pmids_mesh), length(pmids_combined))
)
#>                          query n_pmids
#>                         <char>   <int>
#> 1:                   MeSH only    9999
#> 2: Combined (MeSH + free-text)    9999
cat("Unique to free-text:  ", length(setdiff(pmids_combined, pmids_mesh)), "\n")
#> Unique to free-text:   376
cat("Unique to MeSH:       ", length(setdiff(pmids_mesh, pmids_combined)), "\n")
#> Unique to MeSH:        376
cat("In both:              ", length(intersect(pmids_mesh, pmids_combined)), "\n")
#> In both:               9623

Retrieving records

Fetch abstracts for a subset of the combined result set and inspect which MeSH headings PubMed has assigned.

pmids_sub <- head(pmids_combined, 40L)

abstracts <- puremoe::get_records(
  pmids_sub,
  endpoint = "pubmed_abstracts",
  cores    = 1L,
  sleep    = 0.5
)
assigned_mesh <- data.table::rbindlist(abstracts$annotations)[type == "MeSH"]

assigned_mesh[, .N, by = DescriptorName][order(-N)] |>
  head(20) |>
  DT::datatable(rownames = FALSE)

Persistent storage

Both datasets are ~10 MB. Download once and cache locally to avoid re-fetching each session.

thesaurus <- puremoe::data_mesh_thesaurus(use_persistent_storage = TRUE)
trees     <- puremoe::data_mesh_trees(use_persistent_storage = TRUE)

Summary

data_mesh_thesaurus() → find descriptors → data_mesh_trees() → explore hierarchy → build a [MeSH Terms] query → search_pubmed() → get_records(). MeSH explosion means a single well-chosen heading often retrieves more relevant records than a list of free-text synonyms.

Developed by Jason Timm.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
